resolvedQuestions.text: Canonical workflow YAML schema and family divergence
Presentation ownership, boundary, and typed exceptions
Mode completeness and family default-mode matrix
Doctor route-manifest taxonomy and executable-edge parsing
Contract generation, router thinning, and ownership-label prevention

keyFindings.title: The triad is a topology convention, not a universal schema
keyFindings.summary: Create, design, speckit, and deep use mode-pair assets; doctor and memory require different topology variants.

keyFindings.title: Normalize execution as nodes and edges
keyFindings.summary: Common semantics span stable node identity, action, conditions, dataflow, approval, timeout, failure, and ordering while literal YAML keys diverge.

keyFindings.title: Bindings and placeholders need typed producers and consumers
keyFindings.summary: External inputs, router bindings, node outputs, state paths, and display placeholders must be distinguishable and validated.

keyFindings.title: Topology must be a discriminated union
keyFindings.summary: At least mode-pair, route-manifest, subaction-workflow, and direct-dispatch variants are needed.

keyFindings.title: One contract must feed rendering and validation
keyFindings.summary: Generated prose tables and structural YAML checks must consume the same command record.

keyFindings.title: Presentation authority is asset-owned across families
keyFindings.summary: Topology differences change workflow dispatch, not the single display authority.

keyFindings.title: PRESENTATION BOUNDARY is a capability fence
keyFindings.summary: It enumerates asset-owned surfaces and load-before-render obligations rather than declaring a second owner.

keyFindings.title: memory/search is a bounded router copy
keyFindings.summary: Its inline retrieval envelope is a hard-render reminder sourced from the asset, not router-owned presentation.

keyFindings.title: Typed exceptions preserve asset ownership
keyFindings.summary: Model owner as an asset record and inline exceptions as anchored, rationale-bearing copies with enumerated surfaces.

keyFindings.title: Mislabeled text ownership is generated-prose drift
keyFindings.summary: Routers call .txt assets presentation Markdown; typed media metadata should generate a neutral label.

keyFindings.title: The current mode-pair corpus is complete but unenforced
keyFindings.summary: All 28 workflow-backed routers have both assets and target rows today; inspection is the only guarantee.

keyFindings.title: Defaults are per-command typed policies
keyFindings.summary: Create fixes confirm, design uses input completeness, deep and most speckit ask, while direct topologies use native policies.

keyFindings.title: The mode matrix needs topology and overrides
keyFindings.summary: A family baseline plus command override represents all six families without a synthetic universal default.

keyFindings.title: Completeness and reachability are independent
keyFindings.summary: Every declared mode needs a row and existing target; orphan assets and undeclared routes must also fail.

keyFindings.title: Aliases make mode-to-asset many-to-one
keyFindings.summary: SpecKit unattended aliases intentionally map to auto YAML, so validation must preserve aliases while testing independent mutations.

keyFindings.title: Doctor is a manifest-backed subaction router
keyFindings.summary: Routing source is a subtype—manifest or inline table—within the existing subaction topology.

keyFindings.title: Manifest routing has three typed layers
keyFindings.summary: Router, owner-scoped manifest record, and workflow target are separated by ordered loader gates.

keyFindings.title: Current edge parsing is lexically unsound and incomplete
keyFindings.summary: Raw text admits comment edges and immediate back-reference checks miss longer cycles.

keyFindings.title: Executable edges require a schema registry
keyFindings.summary: Only declared direct, subaction, and workflow fields emit normalized edges with kind and source location.

keyFindings.title: Cycle validation needs strongly connected components
keyFindings.summary: Self-loops and any multi-node executable component fail with an ordered typed path.

keyFindings.title: Generate four sections from one normalized record
keyFindings.summary: Owned assets, presentation boundary, mode/default prose, and execution targets project the same typed relationships.

keyFindings.title: Generated sections need stable replaceable boundaries
keyFindings.summary: Deterministic check-mode rendering coexists with authored gates, binding, and summaries.

keyFindings.title: Router extraction is ownership-first
keyFindings.summary: Hard presentation/workflow triggers force extraction; LOC and section placement are migration signals only.

keyFindings.title: Ownership labels derive from role and media type
keyFindings.summary: Typed text/plain presentation assets render neutral labels and cannot be called Markdown.

keyFindings.title: The deep compiler is a consumer seam
keyFindings.summary: Shared command paths move behind the versioned record while executor-only metadata stays local.

ruledOutDirections.reason: Doctor and memory have intentional non-triad topologies.
Family serializations differ without semantic disagreement.
Would reject the bounded memory/search hard-render reminder.
The asset remains authoritative and contains the canonical envelope.
Families and commands intentionally resolve omission differently.
SpecKit aliases validly share the auto workflow.
Deleting both a mode row and asset leaves no reference to test.
Execution ownership remains subaction routing; routing source is the subtype.
Still confuses inert strings with executable fields and misses ownership.
Cannot detect cycles longer than two nodes.
Length is not ownership; legitimate gates may be long.
Command-specific gates and bindings need a narrow authored surface.

ruledOutDirections.direction: Compiled executor contracts as router source
ruledOutDirections.reason: They aggregate broader runtime authority than router sections.
